  have hr := rent_og_2000
  cases h with
  | inl hEq =>
      apply Or.inl
      simpa [hr] using hEq

  ∀ x : obj, Politician x → ¬ MemberNaziReichstag x

  exact And.left neither_branch

  have hNotVC : ¬ VeryConscious Taylor := A4 Taylor hD
  exact hNotVC hVC

-- Therefore, the second disjunct must hold: Taylor did neither.
theorem taylor_neither : ¬ GrewUpHealthConsciousHome Taylor ∧ ¬ DisregardWellbeing Taylor :=
  Or.resolve_left Tdisj not_case1

axiom obj : Type

-- Constants for specific entities
axiom Colorectal : obj

-- Predicates
axiom Deadly : obj → Prop
axiom LowSurvival : obj → Prop
axiom Severe : obj → Prop
axiom BileDuct : obj → Prop
axiom Cholangiocarcinoma : obj → Prop
axiom MildFlu : obj → Prop

-- Premises
axiom P1 : ∀ x : obj, Deadly x → LowSurvival x                    -- Deadly → Low survival
axiom P2 : ∀ x : obj, Severe x → Deadly x                         -- Severe → Deadly
axiom P3 : ∀ x : obj, BileDuct x → Severe x                       -- Bile duct → Severe
axiom P4 : ∀ x : obj, Cholangiocarcinoma x → BileDuct x           -- Cholangiocarcinoma → Bile duct
axiom P5 : ∀ x : obj, MildFlu x → LowSurvival x                   -- Mild flu → Low survival
axiom P6 : ¬ (BileDuct Colorectal ∧ LowSurvival Colorectal)       -- Colorectal is not both bile duct and low survival

-- Useful derived lemma: bile duct implies low survival
lemma bileduct_low (x : obj) : BileDuct x → LowSurvival x :=
by
  intro hbd
  have hs : Severe x := P3 x hbd

  have h := L

axiom obj : Type

-- Predicates
axiom Vertebrate : obj → Prop
axiom Invertebrate : obj → Prop
axiom Backbone : obj → Prop
axiom ReproduceMF : obj → Prop
axiom Bee : obj → Prop
axiom QueenBee : obj → Prop

-- Individual
axiom Harry : obj

-- Premises
axiom P1 : ∀ x : obj, Invertebrate x ∨ Vertebrate x
axiom P2 : ∀ x : obj, Backbone x → ReproduceMF x
axiom P3 : ∀ x : obj, Vertebrate x → Backbone x
axiom P4 : ∀ x : obj, Bee x → ¬ ReproduceMF x
axiom P5 : ∀ x : obj, QueenBee x → Bee x
axiom P6 : Bee Harry

-- The target statement is refutable from the premises.
theorem not_target_statement :
  ¬(((Vertebrate Harry ∧ Backbone Harry) ∨ (¬Vertebrate Harry ∧ ¬Backbone Harry))
     → (¬Invertebrate Harry ∧ ¬QueenBee Harry)) := by
  intro h
  -- From Bee Harry and P4, Harry does not reproduce by male-and-female mating
  have nRM : ¬ ReproduceMF Harry := P4 Harry P6
  -- Therefore, Harry cannot have a backbone (P2 would force ReproduceMF)
  have nBB : ¬ Backbone Harry := by
    intro hb
    exact nRM (P2 Harry hb)
  -- Therefore, Harry cannot be a vertebrate (P3 would force Backbone)
  have nV : ¬ Vertebrate Harry := by
    intro hv
    exact nBB (P3 Harry hv)
  -- The antecedent holds via the right disjunct (neither vertebrate nor backbone)
  have ante :
    (Vertebrate Harry ∧ Backbone Harry) ∨ (¬Vertebrate Harry ∧ ¬Backbone Harry) :=
    Or.inr (And.intro nV nBB)

axiom Clumsy : obj → Prop
axiom Foodie : obj → Prop
axiom GoesOutFrequently : obj → Prop

-- Premises
axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, Spills x → ¬ Tidy x
axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, (Clumsy x ∧ Foodie x ∧ GoesOutFrequently x) → Spills x
axiom A3 : ∀ x : obj, Cleanly x → Tidy x
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, ValuesOrder x → Cleanly x
axiom A5 : ∀ x : obj, FamilyPrioritizesOrder x → ValuesOrder x

-- Peter and the exclusive-like disjunction
axiom Peter : obj
axiom A6 : (Spills Peter ∧ Cleanly Peter) ∨ (¬ Spills Peter ∧ ¬ Cleanly Peter)

-- Derived consequences from A6 and other premises

-- From (S ∧ C) ∨ (¬S ∧ ¬C) we can derive S ↔ C
theorem spill_iff_cleanly_Peter : Spills Peter ↔ Cleanly Peter := by
  cases A6 with
  | inl h =>
    have hS : Spills Peter := And.left h
    have hC : Cleanly Peter := And.right h
    apply Iff.intro
    · intro _hS; exact hC
    · intro _hC; exact hS
  | inr h =>
    have hNS : ¬ Spills Peter := And.left h
    have hNC : ¬ Cleanly Peter := And.right h
    apply Iff.intro
    · intro hS; exact False.elim (hNS hS)
    · intro hC; exact False.elim (hNC hC)

-- From Cleanly → Tidy and Spills → ¬Tidy and (Spills ↔ Cleanly), we get ¬Cleanly Peter
theorem not_cleanly_Peter : ¬ Cleanly Peter := by
  intro hC
  have t : Tidy Peter := A3 Peter hC
  have s : Spills Peter := Iff.mpr spill_iff_cleanly_Peter hC
  have nt : ¬ Tidy Peter := A1 Peter s
  exact nt t

-- And thus ¬Spills Peter
theorem not_spills_Peter : ¬ Spills Peter := by
  intro hS
  have hC : Cleanly Peter := Iff.mp spill_iff_cleanly_Peter hS
  exact not_cleanly_Peter hC

-- So the second disjunct of A6 must hold
theorem forced_second_disjunct : (¬ Spills Peter ∧ ¬ Cleanly Peter) :=
  And.intro not_spills_Peter not_cleanly_Peter

-- Note: From these premises we cannot derive Tidy Peter.
-- We have shown ¬Cleanly Peter and ¬Spills Peter, and there is no premise implying Tidy without Cleanly.
</lean>

axiom Evocative : obj → Prop
axiom Dreamy : obj → Prop

axiom T4 : ∀ x : obj, Kelly x ∧ Adores x → Evocative x
axiom T5 : ∀ x : obj, Kelly x ∧ Adores x → Dreamy x
axiom T6 : ∀ x : obj, Adores x ∧ Interesting x → Brutalist x ∧ Evocative x

  intro x hx
  have hdisj := T3 x hx
  cases hdisj with
  | inl hz =>
      have hi := T1 x (And.intro hz hx)
      have hbe := T6 x (And.intro hx hi)
      exact Or.inl (And.right hbe)
  | inr hk =>
      have hev := T4 x (And.intro hk hx)
      exact Or.inl hev

def KellyStyle : obj → Prop := fun _ => False

def Brutalist : obj → Prop := fun _ => False

def Dreamy : obj → Prop := fun _ => False

  have hz : ZahaStyle x := And.left hx

  intro x
  intro hx
  intro hi
  have hb : Brutalist x := And.left hx
  exact False.elim hb

-- 3. Every style that Max adores is either Zaha Hadid's design style or Kelly Wearstler's design style.
theorem P3 : ∀ x : obj, (Style x ∧ Adores x) → (ZahaStyle x ∨ KellyStyle x) := by
  intro x
  intro hx
  have hs : Style x := And.left hx
  exact False.elim hs

  intro x
  intro hx
  have hk : KellyStyle x := And.left hx
  exact False.elim hk

  intro x
  intro hx
  have hk : KellyStyle x := And.left hx
  exact False.elim hk

  have hd : DesignByMax x := And.left hx

theorem not_Conclusion : ¬ Conclusion := by
  intro h
  cases h with
  | intro x hx =>
    have hd : DesignByMax x := And.left hx
    exact hd
</lean>

axiom obj : Type

axiom CanBlock : obj → obj → Prop
axiom Jumps : obj → Prop

axiom Great : obj → Prop

axiom Windy : obj

-- If a man is taller than another man, the taller one can block the other's shooting.

axiom M_man : Man Michael

axiom taller_trans : ∀ x y z : obj, Taller x y → Taller y z → Taller x z

axiom P_man : Man Peter
axiom P_taller_M : Taller Peter Michael

-- Michael can block any shooting from a person as long as the person does not jump when shooting.

open Classical

theorem windy_great : Great Windy := by
  have hJ : Jumps Windy := by
    by_contra hnj
    exact not_M_blocks_W (M_blocks_if_not_jump Windy hnj)
  exact great_of_shooter_jump Windy W_shooter hJ
</lean>

<lean>
/- Domain and predicates -/
axiom obj : Type
axiom Michael : obj
axiom Peter : obj
axiom Windy : obj

axiom Man : obj → Prop
axiom Shooter : obj → Prop
axiom GreatShooter : obj → Prop
axiom JumpWhenShooting : obj → Prop
axiom InClass : obj → Prop            -- "in Michael's class"
axiom Taller : obj → obj → Prop       -- Taller x y means x is taller than y
axiom Block : obj → obj → Prop        -- Block x y means x can block y's shooting

/- Background axioms from the text -/
-- If a man is taller than another man, the taller one can block the other's shooting.
axiom blocks_if_taller : ∀ x y : obj, Man x → Man y → Taller x y → Block x y

-- Michael is a man who is taller than everyone else in his class (everyone else ≠ Michael).
axiom Michael_is_man : Man Michael
axiom Michael_taller_than_all_others_in_his_class :
  ∀ y : obj, InClass y → y ≠ Michael → Taller Michael y

-- Transitivity of "taller than".
axiom taller_trans : ∀ x y z : obj, Taller x y → Taller y z → Taller x z

-- Peter is a man who is taller than Michael.
axiom Peter_is_man : Man Peter
axiom Peter_taller_than_Michael : Taller Peter Michael

-- Michael can block any shooting from a person as long as the person does not jump when shooting.
axiom Michael_blocks_nonjumpers : ∀ p : obj, ¬ JumpWhenShooting p → Block Michael p

-- Michael cannot block Windy's shooting.
axiom Michael_cannot_block_Windy : ¬ Block Michael Windy

-- Every shooter who can jump when shooting is a great shooter.
axiom great_if_jump_shooter : ∀ s : obj, Shooter s → JumpWhenShooting s → GreatShooter s

/- Natural properties of "taller than" (implicit in the concept): irreflexivity. -/
axiom taller_irrefl : ∀ x : obj, ¬ Taller x x

/- Derived lemmas -/
theorem taller_asymm : ∀ x y : obj, Taller x y → ¬ Taller y x := by
  intro x y hxy
  intro hyx
  have hxx : Taller x x := taller_trans x y x hxy hyx
  exact (taller_irrefl x) hxx

open Classical

-- If y is in Michael's class and y ≠ Michael, then Peter is taller than y.
theorem peter_taller_than_classmate_ne_Michael :
  ∀ y : obj, InClass y → y ≠ Michael → Taller Peter y := by
  intro y hy hne
  have hMy : Taller Michael y := Michael_taller_than_all_others_in_his_class y hy hne
  exact taller_trans Peter Michael y Peter_taller_than_Michael hMy

-- Any member of Michael's class is not taller than Peter.
theorem no_class_member_taller_than_Peter : ∀ y : obj, InClass y → ¬ Taller y Peter := by
  intro y hy
  have hcases : y = Michael ∨ y ≠ Michael := Classical.em (y = Michael)
  cases hcases with
  | inl h =>
      -- y = Michael
      have h_asymm : ¬ Taller Michael Peter :=
        taller_asymm Peter Michael Peter_taller_than_Michael
      simpa [h] using h_asymm
  | inr hne =>
      -- y ≠ Michael
      have hPy : Taller Peter y := peter_taller_than_classmate_ne_Michael y hy hne
      exact taller_asymm Peter y hPy

-- Therefore, there is no man in Michael's class who is taller than Peter.
theorem no_man_in_Michaels_class_taller_than_Peter :
  ¬ ∃ m : obj, Man m ∧ InClass m ∧ Taller m Peter := by
  intro h
  cases h with
  | intro m hm =>
    cases hm with
    | intro hm_man hm' =>
      cases hm' with
      | intro hm_in hm_taller =>
        have hnot : ¬ Taller m Peter := no_class_member_taller_than_Peter m hm_in
        exact hnot hm_taller
</lean>

axiom Man : obj → Prop
axiom Shooter : obj → Prop
axiom GreatShooter : obj → Prop
axiom JumpWhenShooting : obj → Prop
axiom InClass : obj → Prop            -- "in Michael's class"
axiom Taller : obj → obj → Prop       -- Taller x y means x is taller than y
axiom Block : obj → obj → Prop        -- Block x y means x can block y's shooting

/- Background axioms from the text -/
-- If a man is taller than another man, the taller one can block the other's shooting.
axiom blocks_if_taller : ∀ x y : obj, Man x → Man y → Taller x y → Block x y

-- Michael is a man who is taller than everyone else in his class (everyone else ≠ Michael).
axiom Michael_is_man : Man Michael
axiom Michael_taller_than_all_others_in_his_class :
  ∀ y : obj, InClass y → y ≠ Michael → Taller Michael y

-- Transitivity of "taller than".
axiom taller_trans : ∀ x y z : obj, Taller x y → Taller y z → Taller x z

-- Peter is a man who is taller than Michael.
axiom Peter_is_man : Man Peter
axiom Peter_taller_than_Michael : Taller Peter Michael

-- Michael can block any shooting from a person as long as the person does not jump when shooting.
axiom Michael_blocks_nonjumpers : ∀ p : obj, ¬ JumpWhenShooting p → Block Michael p

-- Michael cannot block Windy's shooting.
axiom Michael_cannot_block_Windy : ¬ Block Michael Windy

-- Every shooter who can jump when shooting is a great shooter.
axiom great_if_jump_shooter : ∀ s : obj, Shooter s → JumpWhenShooting s → GreatShooter s

/- Natural properties of "taller than" (implicit in the concept): irreflexivity. -/
axiom taller_irrefl : ∀ x : obj, ¬ Taller x x

/- Derived lemmas -/
theorem taller_asymm : ∀ x y : obj, Taller x y → ¬ Taller y x := by
  intro x y hxy
  intro hyx
  have hxx : Taller x x := taller_trans x y x hxy hyx
  exact (taller_irrefl x) hxx

open Classical

-- If y is in Michael's class and y ≠ Michael, then Peter is taller than y.
theorem peter_taller_than_classmate_ne_Michael :
  ∀ y : obj, InClass y → y ≠ Michael → Taller Peter y := by
  intro y hy hne
  have hMy : Taller Michael y := Michael_taller_than_all_others_in_his_class y hy hne
  exact taller_trans Peter Michael y Peter_taller_than_Michael hMy

-- Any member of Michael's class is not taller than Peter.
theorem no_class_member_taller_than_Peter : ∀ y : obj, InClass y → ¬ Taller y Peter := by
  intro y hy
  have hcases : y = Michael ∨ y ≠ Michael := Classical.em (y = Michael)
  cases hcases with
  | inl h =>
      -- y = Michael
      have h_asymm : ¬ Taller Michael Peter :=
        taller_asymm Peter Michael Peter_taller_than_Michael
      simpa [h] using h_asymm
  | inr hne =>
      -- y ≠ Michael
      have hPy : Taller Peter y := peter_taller_than_classmate_ne_Michael y hy hne
      exact taller_asymm Peter y hPy

-- Therefore, there is no man in Michael's class who is taller than Peter.
theorem no_man_in_Michaels_class_taller_than_Peter :
  ¬ ∃ m : obj, Man m ∧ InClass m ∧ Taller m Peter := by
  intro h
  cases h with
  | intro m hm =>
    cases hm with
    | intro hm_man hm' =>
      cases hm' with
      | intro hm_in hm_taller =>
        have hnot : ¬ Taller m Peter := no_class_member_taller_than_Peter m hm_in
        exact hnot hm_taller

-- If a man is taller than another man, the taller one can block the other's shooting.

axiom taller_trans : ∀ x y z : obj, Taller x y → Taller y z → Taller x z

-- Michael can block any shooting from a person as long as the person does not jump when shooting.

axiom Michael_cannot_block_Windy : ¬ CanBlock Michael Windy

-- Every shooter who can jump when shooting is a great shooter.
axiom great_if_jump_shooter : ∀ x : obj, JumpsWhenShooting x → GreatShooter x

-- A useful derived fact: Windy must jump when shooting (by contraposition on Michael's blocking rule).
lemma Windy_jumps : JumpsWhenShooting Windy := by
  classical
  by_contra h
  have hb : CanBlock Michael Windy := Michael_blocks_if_no_jump Windy h
  exact Michael_cannot_block_Windy hb

-- Peter can block Michael (but this does not yield the desired conclusion about Windy).
lemma Peter_blocks_Michael : CanBlock Peter Michael :=
  R1 Peter Michael Peter_man Michael_man Peter_taller_Michael
</lean>

axiom obj : Type

axiom Jane : obj
axiom KiKi : obj

axiom Animal : obj → Prop
axiom Bark : obj → Prop
axiom Dog : obj → Prop
axiom Jump : obj → Prop
axiom Legs : obj → Prop
axiom Terricolous : obj → Prop
axiom Likes : obj → obj → Prop

-- Jane does not like any animals that bark.
axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, Animal x ∧ Bark x → ¬ Likes Jane x
-- All dogs are animals that bark.
axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, Dog x → Animal x ∧ Bark x
-- Jane likes all animals that jump.
axiom A3 : ∀ x : obj, Animal x ∧ Jump x → Likes Jane x
-- If an animal has legs, then it jumps.
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, Animal x ∧ Legs x → Jump x
-- If an animal is terricolous, then it has legs.
axiom A5 : ∀ x : obj, Animal x ∧ Terricolous x → Legs x
-- KiKi is an animal.
axiom A6 : Animal KiKi
-- If KiKi neither jumps nor has legs, then KiKi is terricolous or has legs.
axiom A7 : (¬ Jump KiKi ∧ ¬ Legs KiKi) → (Terricolous KiKi ∨ Legs KiKi)

-- Some derivable consequences (but none suffice to prove Terricolous KiKi):

-- Terricolous animals (being animals) have legs, hence jump, hence are liked by Jane.
theorem terricolous_animals_liked_by_Jane :
  ∀ x : obj, Animal x ∧ Terricolous x → Likes Jane x :=
by
  intro x
  intro h
  have hlegs : Legs x := A5 x h
  have hjump : Jump x :=
    by
      apply A4 x
      apply And.intro
      · exact h.left
      · exact hlegs
  have hAJ : Animal x ∧ Jump x :=
    by
      apply And.intro
      · exact h.left
      · exact hjump
  exact A3 x hAJ

-- If KiKi is terricolous, then KiKi has legs.
theorem kiki_terricolous_implies_legs : Terricolous KiKi → Legs KiKi :=
by
  intro ht
  have hA : Animal KiKi ∧ Terricolous KiKi :=
    by
      apply And.intro
      · exact A6
      · exact ht
  exact A5 KiKi hA
</lean>

axiom obj : Type
axiom JohnCar : obj

axiom TransportsMulti : obj → Prop
axiom OneSeater : obj → Prop
axiom TeslaModel3 : obj → Prop
axiom SingleSeatElectricCar : obj → Prop
axiom SoloEV : obj → Prop

-- If something can transport multiple passengers, then they are not one-seaters.
axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, TransportsMulti x → ¬ OneSeater x
-- All Tesla Model 3 can transport multiple passengers.
axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, TeslaModel3 x → TransportsMulti x
-- All single-seat electric cars are one-seaters.
axiom A3 : ∀ x : obj, SingleSeatElectricCar x → OneSeater x
-- All Solo EV cars are single-seat electric cars.
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, SoloEV x → SingleSeatElectricCar x
-- If John's car is not a Tesla Model 3, then it's not a Tesla Model 3 or a single-seat electric car.
axiom A5 : ¬ TeslaModel3 JohnCar → (¬ TeslaModel3 JohnCar ∨ SingleSeatElectricCar JohnCar)

-- Derived: Tesla Model 3 implies not one-seater.
theorem T3_not_OneSeater : ∀ x : obj, TeslaModel3 x → ¬ OneSeater x := by
  intro x hT3
  have hTM : TransportsMulti x := A2 x hT3
  exact A1 x hTM

-- Derived: SoloEV implies one-seater.
theorem SoloEV_is_OneSeater : ∀ x : obj, SoloEV x → OneSeater x := by
  intro x hSolo
  have hSSE : SingleSeatElectricCar x := A4 x hSolo
  exact A3 x hSSE

-- Incompatibility: A Tesla Model 3 cannot be a SoloEV.
theorem T3_not_SoloEV : ∀ x : obj, TeslaModel3 x → ¬ SoloEV x := by
  intro x hT3 hSolo
  have ho : OneSeater x := SoloEV_is_OneSeater x hSolo
  have hn : ¬ OneSeater x := T3_not_OneSeater x hT3
  exact hn ho

-- Symmetrically: A SoloEV cannot be a Tesla Model 3.

axiom Breathe : obj → Prop
axiom HasWings : obj → Prop

-- All of Peter's pets that are mammals are also animals.
axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, Pet x → Mammal x → Animal x
-- All monkeys are mammals.
axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, Monkey x → Mammal x
-- Peter's pets are all either monkeys or birds.
axiom A3 : ∀ x : obj, Pet x → Monkey x ∨ Bird x
-- Peter's birds can fly.
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, Pet x → Bird x → CanFly x
-- All animals that are Peter's pets can breathe.
axiom A5 : ∀ x : obj, Animal x → Pet x → Breathe x
-- If Peter's pet can fly, then it has wings.
axiom A6 : ∀ x : obj, Pet x → CanFly x → HasWings x
-- Rock is Peter's pet.
axiom A7 : Pet Rock
-- Rock can fly, or Rock is a bird, or Rock cannot breathe.
axiom A8 : (CanFly Rock ∨ Bird Rock) ∨ ¬Breathe Rock

theorem Rock_has_wings : HasWings Rock :=
by
  have pRock : Pet Rock := A7
  cases A8 with
  | inl h =>
      cases h with
      | inl fly =>
          exact A6 Rock pRock fly
      | inr bird =>
          have fly : CanFly Rock := A4 Rock pRock bird
          exact A6 Rock pRock fly
  | inr nbreathe =>

axiom Terricolous : obj → Prop
axiom Likes : obj → obj → Prop

-- Jane does not like any animals that bark.
axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, Animal x → Bark x → ¬ Likes Jane x
-- All dogs are animals that bark.
axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, Dog x → Animal x ∧ Bark x
-- Jane likes all animals that jump.
axiom A3 : ∀ x : obj, Animal x → Jump x → Likes Jane x
-- If an animal has legs, then it jumps.
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, Animal x → Legs x → Jump x
-- If an animal is terricolous, then it has legs.
axiom A5 : ∀ x : obj, Animal x → Terricolous x → Legs x
-- KiKi is an animal.
axiom A6 : Animal KiKi
-- If KiKi neither jumps nor has legs, then KiKi is terricolous or has legs.
axiom A7 : (¬ Jump KiKi ∧ ¬ Legs KiKi) → (Terricolous KiKi ∨ Legs KiKi)

theorem kiki_not_bark_and_not_dog : ¬ Bark KiKi ∧ ¬ Dog KiKi := by
  have hA : Animal KiKi := A6
  -- First, show KiKi does not bark.
  have nbark : ¬ Bark KiKi := by
    intro hbark
    -- From barking, Jane does not like KiKi.
    have hnotlike : ¬ Likes Jane KiKi := A1 KiKi hA hbark
    -- From A3, if KiKi jumps then Jane likes KiKi; thus ¬like implies ¬jump.
    have hj_to_like : Jump KiKi → Likes Jane KiKi := A3 KiKi hA
    have hnotjump : ¬ Jump KiKi := fun hj => hnotlike (hj_to_like hj)
    -- From A4, legs imply jump; thus ¬jump implies ¬legs.
    have hnotlegs : ¬ Legs KiKi := fun hl => hnotjump (A4 KiKi hA hl)
    -- Apply A7 to derive a contradiction.
    have t_or_l : Terricolous KiKi ∨ Legs KiKi := A7 ⟨hnotjump, hnotlegs⟩
    have hlegs : Legs KiKi := by
      cases t_or_l with
      | inl ht => exact A5 KiKi hA ht
      | inr hl => exact hl
    exact hnotlegs hlegs
  -- Next, show KiKi is not a dog (since all dogs bark).
  have ndog : ¬ Dog KiKi := by
    intro hdog
    have hbark : Bark KiKi := (A2 KiKi hdog).right
    exact nbark hbark
  exact And.intro nbark ndog
</lean>

axiom Animal : obj → Prop

axiom Dog : obj → Prop

axiom Terricolous : obj → Prop
axiom Likes : obj → obj → Prop

-- Jane does not like any animals that bark.
axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, Animal x → Bark x → ¬ Likes Jane x
-- All dogs are animals that bark.
axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, Dog x → Animal x ∧ Bark x
-- Jane likes all animals that jump.
axiom A3 : ∀ x : obj, Animal x → Jump x → Likes Jane x
-- If an animal has legs, then it jumps.
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, Animal x → Legs x → Jump x
-- If an animal is terricolous, then it has legs.
axiom A5 : ∀ x : obj, Animal x → Terricolous x → Legs x
-- KiKi is an animal.
axiom A6 : Animal KiKi
-- If KiKi neither jumps nor has legs, then KiKi is terricolous or has legs.
axiom A7 : (¬ Jump KiKi ∧ ¬ Legs KiKi) → (Terricolous KiKi ∨ Legs KiKi)

theorem kiki_not_bark_and_not_dog : ¬ Bark KiKi ∧ ¬ Dog KiKi := by
  have hA : Animal KiKi := A6
  -- First, show KiKi does not bark.
  have nbark : ¬ Bark KiKi := by
    intro hbark
    -- From barking, Jane does not like KiKi.
    have hnotlike : ¬ Likes Jane KiKi := A1 KiKi hA hbark
    -- From A3, if KiKi jumps then Jane likes KiKi; thus ¬like implies ¬jump.
    have hj_to_like : Jump KiKi → Likes Jane KiKi := A3 KiKi hA
    have hnotjump : ¬ Jump KiKi := fun hj => hnotlike (hj_to_like hj)
    -- From A4, legs imply jump; thus ¬jump implies ¬legs.
    have hnotlegs : ¬ Legs KiKi := fun hl => hnotjump (A4 KiKi hA hl)
    -- Apply A7 to derive a contradiction.
    have t_or_l : Terricolous KiKi ∨ Legs KiKi := A7 ⟨hnotjump, hnotlegs⟩
    have hlegs : Legs KiKi := by
      cases t_or_l with
      | inl ht => exact A5 KiKi hA ht
      | inr hl => exact hl
    exact hnotlegs hlegs
  -- Next, show KiKi is not a dog (since all dogs bark).
  have ndog : ¬ Dog KiKi := by
    intro hdog
    have hbark : Bark KiKi := (A2 KiKi hdog).right
    exact nbark hbark
  exact And.intro nbark ndog

axiom LargerFamily : obj → Prop
axiom InviteFriends : obj → Prop

-- All people who make wedding plans are engaged.
axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, MakePlans x → Engaged x
-- All people who invite others to come to their ceremony make wedding plans.
axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, InviteOthers x → MakePlans x
-- Anyone who has a well-attended wedding invites others to come to their ceremony.
axiom A3 : ∀ x : obj, WellAttended x → InviteOthers x
-- Some people that have well-attended weddings have larger families.
axiom A4 : ∃ x : obj, WellAttended x ∧ LargerFamily x
-- If John is engaged, then John does not invite friends to their ceremony and make wedding plans.
axiom A5 : Engaged John → (¬ InviteFriends John ∧ MakePlans John)
-- If John has a larger family, then either John has a well-attended wedding or invites friends to their ceremony.
axiom A6 : LargerFamily John → (WellAttended John ∨ InviteFriends John)

theorem wellAttended_implies_makePlans : ∀ x : obj, WellAttended x → MakePlans x :=
  fun x h => A2 x (A3 x h)

theorem wellAttended_implies_engaged : ∀ x : obj, WellAttended x → Engaged x :=
  fun x h => A1 x (wellAttended_implies_makePlans x h)

  fun h => (A5 h).left

  fun h => (A5 h).right

by
  apply Iff.intro
  · intro h; exact engaged_implies_makePlans h

  Engaged John → LargerFamily John → WellAttended John :=
by
  intro heng hlf
  have hdisj := A6 hlf
  cases hdisj with
  | inl hw => exact hw
  | inr hf =>
      have hnf := engaged_implies_not_inviteFriends heng
      exact False.elim (hnf hf)

axiom Unsupervised : Alg → Prop
axiom Reinforcement : Alg → Prop
axiom RequiresLabels : Alg → Prop

-- Categorization: any ML algorithm is either supervised, unsupervised, or reinforcement
axiom categorize : ∀ a : Alg, ML a → Supervised a ∨ Unsupervised a ∨ Reinforcement a

axiom used_implies_ML : ∀ a : Alg, UsedToTrain a M → ML a

-- Reinforcement learning is not used to train the model
axiom rl_not_used : ∀ a : Alg, UsedToTrain a M → ¬ Reinforcement a

-- The ML algorithm used to train the model requires labeled data
axiom label_required : ∀ a : Alg, UsedToTrain a M → RequiresLabels a

theorem supervised_used : ∃ a : Alg, UsedToTrain a M ∧ Supervised a := by
  cases ex_used with
  | intro a hUsed =>
    have hML : ML a := used_implies_ML a hUsed
    have hReq : RequiresLabels a := label_required a hUsed

    have hCat : Supervised a ∨ Unsupervised a ∨ Reinforcement a := categorize a hML
    cases hCat with
    | inl hSup =>
        exact Exists.intro a (And.intro hUsed hSup)
    | inr hRest =>
        cases hRest with
        | inl hU =>

        | inr hR =>
            exact False.elim (hNotRL hR)
</lean>

axiom obj : Type

-- Predicates
axiom InClub : obj → Prop
axiom PerformOften : obj → Prop
axiom AttendsEvents : obj → Prop
axiom EngagedEvents : obj → Prop
axiom InactiveDisinterested : obj → Prop
axiom ChaperoneDances : obj → Prop
axiom StudentAtSchool : obj → Prop
axiom YoungChild : obj → Prop
axiom Teenager : obj → Prop
axiom WishFurtherAcademic : obj → Prop

-- Premises
-- 1. Club members who perform often attend and are very engaged with school events.
axiom P1 : ∀ x : obj, InClub x → (PerformOften x → (AttendsEvents x ∧ EngagedEvents x))
-- 2. Club members either perform often or are inactive and disinterested.
axiom P2 : ∀ x : obj, InClub x → (PerformOften x ∨ InactiveDisinterested x)
-- 3. Club members who chaperone high school dances are not students at the school.
axiom P3 : ∀ x : obj, (InClub x ∧ ChaperoneDances x) → ¬ StudentAtSchool x
-- 4. Inactive and disinterested club members chaperone high school dances.
axiom P4 : ∀ x : obj, (InClub x ∧ InactiveDisinterested x) → ChaperoneDances x
-- 5. Young children or teenagers in the club who wish to further academics are students at the school.
axiom P5 : ∀ x : obj, (InClub x ∧ (YoungChild x ∨ Teenager x) ∧ WishFurtherAcademic x) → StudentAtSchool x

-- Bonnie and her status
axiom Bonnie : obj
axiom B_inClub : InClub Bonnie
-- Bonnie either (attends and is very engaged and is a student) or (not(attends and engaged) and not a student).
axiom B_choice :
  (AttendsEvents Bonnie ∧ EngagedEvents Bonnie ∧ StudentAtSchool Bonnie) ∨
  (¬ (AttendsEvents Bonnie ∧ EngagedEvents Bonnie) ∧ ¬ StudentAtSchool Bonnie)

-- A useful lemma: If Bonnie is a student at the school, then she performs often.
theorem bonnie_performs_if_student : StudentAtSchool Bonnie → PerformOften Bonnie := by
  intro hS
  have hC : InClub Bonnie := B_inClub
  have h := P2 Bonnie hC
  cases h with
  | inl hPerf => exact hPerf
  | inr hInact =>
    have hChap : ChaperoneDances Bonnie := P4 Bonnie (And.intro hC hInact)
    have hNotS : ¬ StudentAtSchool Bonnie := P3 Bonnie (And.intro hC hChap)

  have hNotPicky : ¬ Picky Hannah := And.left (hCond hNeed)

<lean>
axiom obj : Type

-- Predicates
axiom Club : obj → Prop
axiom PerformOften : obj → Prop
axiom AttendEngaged : obj → Prop
axiom InactiveDisinterested : obj → Prop
axiom Chaperone : obj → Prop
axiom Student : obj → Prop
axiom YoungOrTeen : obj → Prop
axiom WishFurtherAcademic : obj → Prop

-- Individual
axiom Bonnie : obj

-- Premises from the context
-- 1. People in this club who perform often attend and are very engaged with school events.
axiom P1 : ∀ x : obj, Club x ∧ PerformOften x → AttendEngaged x
-- 2. People in this club either perform often or are inactive and disinterested community members.
axiom P2 : ∀ x : obj, Club x → PerformOften x ∨ InactiveDisinterested x
-- 3. People in this club who chaperone high school dances are not students who attend the school.
axiom P3 : ∀ x : obj, Club x ∧ Chaperone x → ¬ Student x
-- 4. All people in this club who are inactive and disinterested members of their community chaperone high school dances.
axiom P4 : ∀ x : obj, Club x ∧ InactiveDisinterested x → Chaperone x
-- 5. All young children and teenagers in this club who wish to further their academic careers are students who attend the school.
axiom P5 : ∀ x : obj, Club x ∧ YoungOrTeen x ∧ WishFurtherAcademic x → Student x
-- 6. Bonnie is in this club and she either (attends & is very engaged with events and is a student) or (does not attend & is not a student).
axiom BonClub : Club Bonnie
axiom BonAE_iff_S : (AttendEngaged Bonnie ∧ Student Bonnie) ∨ (¬ AttendEngaged Bonnie ∧ ¬ Student Bonnie)

-- Some derived facts (showing attempts)
theorem bonnie_chaperone_or_perform : Chaperone Bonnie ∨ PerformOften Bonnie := by
  have h := P2 Bonnie BonClub
  cases h with
  | inl hPerf =>
      exact Or.inr hPerf
  | inr hInact =>
      have hChap : Chaperone Bonnie := P4 Bonnie (And.intro BonClub hInact)
      exact Or.inl hChap

theorem antecedent_holds : Chaperone Bonnie ∨ (¬ Chaperone Bonnie → PerformOften Bonnie) := by
  have h := bonnie_chaperone_or_perform
  cases h with
  | inl hChap =>
      exact Or.inl hChap
  | inr hPerf =>
      apply Or.inr
      intro _
      exact hPerf

theorem chaperone_implies_not_student : Chaperone Bonnie → ¬ Student Bonnie := by
  intro hChap
  exact P3 Bonnie (And.intro BonClub hChap)

theorem not_student_implies_not_attend : ¬ Student Bonnie → ¬ AttendEngaged Bonnie := by
  intro hNotS
  intro hA
  have h := BonAE_iff_S
  cases h with
  | inl hAS =>
      exact hNotS (And.right hAS)
  | inr hNotANotS =>
      exact (And.left hNotANotS) hA

theorem chaperone_implies_not_attend : Chaperone Bonnie → ¬ AttendEngaged Bonnie := by
  intro hChap
  apply not_student_implies_not_attend
  exact chaperone_implies_not_student hChap

theorem not_chaperone_implies_attend : ¬ Chaperone Bonnie → AttendEngaged Bonnie := by
  intro hNotChap
  -- From antecedent_holds, if not chaperone then perform often; hence attend (since Bonnie is in the club).
  have hAnte := antecedent_holds
  have hPerf : PerformOften Bonnie := by
    cases hAnte with
    | inl hChap => exact False.elim (hNotChap hChap)
    | inr hImp => exact hImp hNotChap
  exact P1 Bonnie (And.intro BonClub hPerf)

theorem attend_implies_student : AttendEngaged Bonnie → Student Bonnie := by
  intro hA
  cases BonAE_iff_S with
  | inl hAS =>
      exact And.right hAS
  | inr hNotANotS =>
      have : False := (And.left hNotANotS) hA
      exact False.elim this
</lean>

<lean>
axiom obj : Type

-- Individuals
axiom James : obj

-- Predicates
axiom Employee : obj → Prop
axiom Manager : obj → Prop
axiom LunchCompany : obj → Prop
axiom LunchHome : obj → Prop
axiom ScheduleMeeting : obj → Prop
axiom GoCompanyToday : obj → Prop
axiom Remote : obj → Prop
axiom InOtherCountries : obj → Prop

-- Premises from the context
-- All employees who schedule a meeting with their customers will go to the company building today.
axiom P1 : ∀ x : obj, Employee x → ScheduleMeeting x → GoCompanyToday x
-- Everyone who has lunch in the company building schedules meetings with their customers.
axiom P2 : ∀ x : obj, LunchCompany x → ScheduleMeeting x
-- Employees have lunch either in the company building or at home.
axiom P3 : ∀ x : obj, Employee x → (LunchCompany x ∨ LunchHome x)
-- If an employee has lunch at home, they are working remotely from home. (We model just lunch-at-home implies remote)
axiom P4 : ∀ x : obj, LunchHome x → Remote x
-- All employees who are in other countries work remotely from home.
axiom P5 : ∀ x : obj, Employee x → InOtherCountries x → Remote x
-- No managers work remotely from home.
axiom P6 : ∀ x : obj, Manager x → ¬ Remote x
-- James will appear in the company today iff he is a manager. (identify "appear" with "go to the company today")
axiom P7 : GoCompanyToday James ↔ Manager James

-- Some derived lemmas (showing what would follow under additional assumptions)

-- If James is an employee and a manager, then he has lunch in the company.
theorem manager_employee_lunch_company :
  Employee James → Manager James → LunchCompany James := by
  intro eJ mJ
  have notRemote : ¬ Remote James := P6 James mJ
  have notLunchHome : ¬ LunchHome James := by
    intro lh
    have r : Remote James := P4 James lh
    exact notRemote r
  have disj : LunchCompany James ∨ LunchHome James := P3 James eJ
  cases disj with
  | inl lc => exact lc
  | inr lh => exact False.elim (notLunchHome lh)

-- If James is an employee and not a manager, then he has lunch at home.
theorem not_manager_employee_lunch_home :
  Employee James → ¬ Manager James → LunchHome James := by
  intro eJ notM
  -- From ¬Manager and (Go ↔ Manager), get ¬Go
  have notGo : ¬ GoCompanyToday James := by
    apply mt (Iff.mp P7)
    exact notM
  -- For employees, LunchCompany implies GoCompanyToday
  have lunchCompany_implies_go : LunchCompany James → GoCompanyToday James := by
    intro lc
    have sm : ScheduleMeeting James := P2 James lc
    exact P1 James eJ sm
  -- So ¬Go implies ¬LunchCompany
  have notLunchCompany : ¬ LunchCompany James := by
    intro lc
    exact notGo (lunchCompany_implies_go lc)
  -- Use the employee lunch dichotomy
  have disj : LunchCompany James ∨ LunchHome James := P3 James eJ
  cases disj with
  | inl lc => exact False.elim (notLunchCompany lc)
  | inr lh => exact lh
</lean>

axiom obj : Type

-- Individuals
axiom James : obj

-- Predicates
axiom Employee : obj → Prop
axiom Manager : obj → Prop
axiom LunchCompany : obj → Prop
axiom LunchHome : obj → Prop
axiom ScheduleMeeting : obj → Prop
axiom GoCompanyToday : obj → Prop
axiom Remote : obj → Prop
axiom InOtherCountries : obj → Prop

-- Premises from the context
-- All employees who schedule a meeting with their customers will go to the company building today.
axiom P1 : ∀ x : obj, Employee x → ScheduleMeeting x → GoCompanyToday x
-- Everyone who has lunch in the company building schedules meetings with their customers.
axiom P2 : ∀ x : obj, LunchCompany x → ScheduleMeeting x
-- Employees have lunch either in the company building or at home.
axiom P3 : ∀ x : obj, Employee x → (LunchCompany x ∨ LunchHome x)
-- If an employee has lunch at home, they are working remotely from home. (We model just lunch-at-home implies remote)
axiom P4 : ∀ x : obj, LunchHome x → Remote x
-- All employees who are in other countries work remotely from home.
axiom P5 : ∀ x : obj, Employee x → InOtherCountries x → Remote x
-- No managers work remotely from home.
axiom P6 : ∀ x : obj, Manager x → ¬ Remote x
-- James will appear in the company today iff he is a manager. (identify "appear" with "go to the company today")
axiom P7 : GoCompanyToday James ↔ Manager James

-- Some derived lemmas (showing what would follow under additional assumptions)

-- If James is an employee and a manager, then he has lunch in the company.
theorem manager_employee_lunch_company :
  Employee James → Manager James → LunchCompany James := by
  intro eJ mJ
  have notRemote : ¬ Remote James := P6 James mJ
  have notLunchHome : ¬ LunchHome James := by
    intro lh
    have r : Remote James := P4 James lh
    exact notRemote r
  have disj : LunchCompany James ∨ LunchHome James := P3 James eJ
  cases disj with
  | inl lc => exact lc
  | inr lh => exact False.elim (notLunchHome lh)

-- If James is an employee and not a manager, then he has lunch at home.
theorem not_manager_employee_lunch_home :
  Employee James → ¬ Manager James → LunchHome James := by
  intro eJ notM
  -- From ¬Manager and (Go ↔ Manager), get ¬Go
  have notGo : ¬ GoCompanyToday James := by
    apply mt (Iff.mp P7)
    exact notM
  -- For employees, LunchCompany implies GoCompanyToday
  have lunchCompany_implies_go : LunchCompany James → GoCompanyToday James := by
    intro lc
    have sm : ScheduleMeeting James := P2 James lc
    exact P1 James eJ sm
  -- So ¬Go implies ¬LunchCompany
  have notLunchCompany : ¬ LunchCompany James := by
    intro lc
    exact notGo (lunchCompany_implies_go lc)
  -- Use the employee lunch dichotomy
  have disj : LunchCompany James ∨ LunchHome James := P3 James eJ
  cases disj with
  | inl lc => exact False.elim (notLunchCompany lc)
  | inr lh => exact lh

axiom GoToBuildingToday : obj → Prop

axiom LunchAtHome : obj → Prop
axiom RemoteFromHome : obj → Prop
axiom InOtherCountries : obj → Prop
axiom Manager : obj → Prop
axiom AppearsInCompanyToday : obj → Prop

axiom James : obj

-- Premises
-- 1. All employees who schedule a meeting with their customers will go to the company building today.
axiom P1 : ∀ x : obj, Employee x ∧ SchedulesMeeting x → GoToBuildingToday x

-- 2. Everyone who has lunch in the company building schedules meetings with their customers.
axiom P2 : ∀ x : obj, LunchInBuilding x → SchedulesMeeting x

-- 3. Employees have lunch either in the company building or at home.
axiom P3 : ∀ x : obj, Employee x → (LunchInBuilding x ∨ LunchAtHome x)

-- 4. If an employee has lunch at home, they are working remotely from home.
axiom P4 : ∀ x : obj, Employee x ∧ LunchAtHome x → RemoteFromHome x

-- 5. All employees who are in other countries work remotely from home.
axiom P5 : ∀ x : obj, Employee x ∧ InOtherCountries x → RemoteFromHome x

-- 6. No managers work remotely from home.
axiom P6 : ∀ x : obj, Manager x → ¬ RemoteFromHome x

-- 7. James will appear in the company today iff he is a manager.
axiom P7 : AppearsInCompanyToday James ↔ Manager James

-- then they are not a manager.
theorem remote_implies_not_manager (x : obj) : RemoteFromHome x → ¬ Manager x :=
fun rh m => P6 x m rh

-- Specializing to James:
theorem james_remote_implies_not_manager : RemoteFromHome James → ¬ Manager James :=
remote_implies_not_manager James

-- If James is an employee and has lunch at home or works remotely, then he is not a manager.
theorem james_lunch_or_remote_implies_not_manager_given_employee
  (hE : Employee James) :
  (LunchAtHome James ∨ RemoteFromHome James) → ¬ Manager James :=
by
  intro h
  intro hM
  cases h with
  | inl hL =>
      have hR : RemoteFromHome James := P4 James ⟨hE, hL⟩
      exact (P6 James hM) hR
  | inr hR =>
      exact (P6 James hM) hR

-- If James is an employee and in other countries, then he is not a manager (since that implies remote).
theorem james_other_countries_implies_not_manager_given_employee
  (hE : Employee James) (hOC : InOtherCountries James) : ¬ Manager James :=
by
  intro hM
  have hR : RemoteFromHome James := P5 James ⟨hE, hOC⟩
  exact (P6 James hM) hR
</lean>

axiom obj : Type
axiom mixture : obj

axiom Hydrocarbon : obj → Prop
axiom Organic : obj → Prop
axiom Alkane : obj → Prop
axiom Chemical : obj → Prop
axiom ContainsCarbon : obj → Prop
axiom SingleElement : obj → Prop

-- All hydrocarbons are organic compounds.
axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, Hydrocarbon x → Organic x
-- All alkanes are hydrocarbons.
axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, Alkane x → Hydrocarbon x
-- All organic compounds are chemical compounds.
axiom A3 : ∀ x : obj, Organic x → Chemical x
-- All organic compounds contain carbon.
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, Organic x → ContainsCarbon x
-- No chemical compounds contain only one element.
axiom A5 : ∀ x : obj, Chemical x → ¬ SingleElement x
-- The mixture either both is a chemical compound and contains only one element, or neither.
axiom A6 : (Chemical mixture ∧ SingleElement mixture) ∨ (¬ Chemical mixture ∧ ¬ SingleElement mixture)

theorem target :
  (SingleElement mixture ∨ ContainsCarbon mixture) → (¬ Chemical mixture ∧ ¬ Alkane mixture) := by
  intro _
  -- From A6 and A5, deduce that the mixture is neither a chemical compound nor single-element.
  have hneither : ¬ Chemical mixture ∧ ¬ SingleElement mixture := by
    cases A6 with
    | inl hboth =>
        have hc : Chemical mixture := hboth.left
        have hs : SingleElement mixture := hboth.right
        have nhs : ¬ SingleElement mixture := A5 mixture hc
        exact False.elim (nhs hs)
    | inr hneither' =>
        exact hneither'
  have nchem : ¬ Chemical mixture := hneither.left
  have nalk : ¬ Alkane mixture := by
    intro halk
    have hhyd : Hydrocarbon mixture := A2 mixture halk
    have horg : Organic mixture := A1 mixture hhyd
    have hchem : Chemical mixture := A3 mixture horg
    exact nchem hchem
  exact And.intro nchem nalk
</lean>

-- All hydrocarbons are organic compounds.
axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, Hydrocarbon x → Organic x
-- All alkanes are hydrocarbons.
axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, Alkane x → Hydrocarbon x
-- All organic compounds are chemical compounds.
axiom A3 : ∀ x : obj, Organic x → Chemical x
-- All organic compounds contain carbon.

-- No chemical compounds contain only one element.

-- The mixture either both is a chemical compound and contains only one element, or neither.

  (SingleElement mixture ∨ ContainsCarbon mixture) → (¬ Chemical mixture ∧ ¬ Alkane mixture) := by
  intro _
  -- From A6 and A5, deduce that the mixture is neither a chemical compound nor single-element.
  have hneither : ¬ Chemical mixture ∧ ¬ SingleElement mixture := by
    cases A6 with
    | inl hboth =>
        have hc : Chemical mixture := hboth.left
        have hs : SingleElement mixture := hboth.right
        have nhs : ¬ SingleElement mixture := A5 mixture hc
        exact False.elim (nhs hs)
    | inr hneither' =>
        exact hneither'
  have nchem : ¬ Chemical mixture := hneither.left
  have nalk : ¬ Alkane mixture := by
    intro halk
    have hhyd : Hydrocarbon mixture := A2 mixture halk
    have horg : Organic mixture := A1 mixture hhyd
    have hchem : Chemical mixture := A3 mixture horg
    exact nchem hchem
  exact And.intro nchem nalk

  intro hSq
  have hSpot : Spotted Rockie := And.left A6
  have hCalm : Calm Rockie := And.right A6
  have hSk : Skittish Rockie := by
    have hAnd : Squirrel Rockie ∧ Spotted Rockie := And.intro hSq hSpot
    exact A5 Rockie hAnd
  have hNotCalm : ¬ Calm Rockie := A4 Rockie hSk
  exact hNotCalm hCalm

-- From "only spotted are rabbits or squirrels" and not_squirrel, deduce Rockie is a rabbit
theorem rabbit_Rockie : Rabbit Rockie := by
  have hSpot : Spotted Rockie := And.left A6
  have hOr : Rabbit Rockie ∨ Squirrel Rockie := A3 Rockie hSpot
  cases hOr with
  | inl hr => exact hr
  | inr hs => exact False.elim (not_squirrel_Rockie hs)

-- Therefore Rockie is cute (rabbit and spotted implies cute)
theorem cute_Rockie : Cute Rockie := by
  have hSpot : Spotted Rockie := And.left A6
  have hRab : Rabbit Rockie := rabbit_Rockie
  exact A1 Rockie (And.intro hRab hSpot)

-- Show that the target implication is refutable given the premises
theorem not_target :
  ¬ ((Cute Rockie ∧ Calm Rockie) → (Skittish Rockie ∧ Turtle Rockie)) := by
  intro hImp
  have hCalm : Calm Rockie := And.right A6
  have hCute : Cute Rockie := cute_Rockie
  have hCons : Skittish Rockie ∧ Turtle Rockie := hImp (And.intro hCute hCalm)
  have hSk : Skittish Rockie := And.left hCons
  have hNotCalm : ¬ Calm Rockie := A4 Rockie hSk
  exact hNotCalm hCalm
